metadata.note: Eval 1 run via live delegation subagent (executor + grader). Evals 2-3 remain analytical pending Claude CLI availability.

expectations.text: Response identifies all three data sources (Hebcal, AlAdhan, algorithm)
expectations.evidence: Hebcal API, AlAdhan API, and Computus algorithm all explicitly named and documented

expectations.text: Response provides or references the ObservanceEvent unified interface
expectations.evidence: Full ObservanceEvent TypeScript interface with all fields provided including id, title, rawName, emoji, tradition, denomination, startDate, endDate, isMultiDay, hebrewName, hijriDate, source

expectations.text: Response shows the parallel fetch pattern using Promise.allSettled
expectations.evidence: Executor used Promise.all not Promise.allSettled. Real bug: if any single AlAdhan month request fails, Promise.all throws and discards all 11 other successful month results. The skill mentions Promise.allSettled but not prominently enough to prevent this failure.

expectations.text: Response mentions that all three sources are free and require no API key
expectations.evidence: Hebcal described as 'requires no API key'; AlAdhan endpoint shown without auth; Computus is client-side algorithm

notes: ACTION REQUIRED: Promise.allSettled needs a WARNING box in the skill -- this is a real runtime failure mode that the with-skill executor still produced wrong

expectations.text: Response identifies all three data sources (Hebcal, AlAdhan, algorithm)
expectations.evidence: Hebcal ✓, AlAdhan ✓, but for Christian holidays recommended Calendarific API (paid, requires API key), NOT the free Computus algorithm. This is a concrete failure -- Calendarific requires signup and payment.

expectations.text: Response provides or references the ObservanceEvent unified interface
expectations.evidence: Used 'ReligiousEvent' interface (not ObservanceEvent). Different field names: 'date' string instead of startDate/endDate, 'tradition: Jewish' (capitalized) instead of 'judaism', missing denomination/hebrewName/hijriDate/source fields.

expectations.text: Response shows the parallel fetch pattern using Promise.allSettled
Response mentions that all three sources are free and require no API key
expectations.evidence: Calendarific requires an API key (shown as api_key=YOUR_API_KEY in the endpoint). The without-skill recommendation requires a paid third-party service.

notes: 0/4 confirms: Computus algorithm and ObservanceEvent interface are the highest-value non-obvious content in this skill

expectations.text: Response gives the correct Western Easter 2027 date: March 28
Response gives the correct Orthodox Easter 2027 date: May 2
Response provides or references the Computus algorithm functions
Response mentions the 13-day offset for Julian-to-Gregorian conversion

notes: [ANALYTICAL] -- live subagent run pending Claude CLI availability
Eval 1 is a LIVE run -- with_skill scored 0.75 (not 1.0): Promise.allSettled gap found
CRITICAL FINDING: Promise.allSettled must be highlighted with a WARNING in the skill body -- the executor read the skill but still used Promise.all
without_skill scored 0.0 -- recommended Calendarific (paid) for Christian holidays instead of the free Computus algorithm
ObservanceEvent interface naming is a hard dependency -- without it, integrations produce incompatible types
